idea-plugin.name: KStateMachine Visual
idea-plugin.description:
Visualizes KStateMachine state machines defined in Kotlin source files.

The plugin statically analyzes Kotlin files in your project, extracts state machine definitions written with the KStateMachine DSL, and renders them as diagrams in a dedicated tool window — without running the code.

Features
  • Detects createStateMachine, createStateMachineBlocking, and createStdLibStateMachine calls automatically as you switch editor tabs.
  • Renders hierarchical (nested) states, parallel regions, history states, choice pseudo-states, and fork/join patterns.
  • Supports dataState, choiceState, and all other KStateMachine state-factory variants.
  • Shows guarded and conditional transitions (transitionConditionally, transitionOn) with all statically resolvable target branches.
  • Displays diagrams in PlantUML and Mermaid formats; lets you copy or export the diagram source.
Links
  • Source code and documentation
  • Issue tracker
  • KStateMachine library
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.0
  • Initial release.
  • Static analysis of Kotlin source files to detect KStateMachine DSL calls (createStateMachine, createStateMachineBlocking, createStdLibStateMachine) without running the code.
  • Diagram rendering in PlantUML and Mermaid formats with copy and export actions.
  • Support for hierarchical states, parallel regions, history states, choice pseudo-states, and fork/join patterns.
  • Guarded and conditional transitions with statically resolved branch targets.
  • UML meta-info annotations (buildUmlMetaInfo, buildCompositeMetaInfo) for labels, descriptions, and notes.
  • Tree view with text search and expand/collapse controls.
  • Compatible with both K1 and K2 Kotlin plugin modes.
